State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legi • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 327-6441 322-6644

June 7, 1983

Ralph D. Morrell, Chairman
Committee to Reform Voting Practices
in the Assembly of California
825 Newgate Way
Dixon, CA 95620

Re: Advice No. A-83-143

Dear Mr. Morrell:

This letter is to confirm our conversation of June 7, 1983.
Our discussion concerned a flier entitled "Legislative Reform
Act" which you submitted to us because you felt that it
contained a false statement concerning the Legislative Reform
Act of 1983.

I advised you on the telephone that there are no provisions
of the Political Reform Act that regulate the content of such a
flier. The Act's only "content-related" provision requires the
senders of mass mailings to be identified on the mailing.
(Government Code Section 84305.)

Thank you for discussing this matter with our office.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:plh

COMMITTEE TO REFORM VOTING PRACTICES
IN
THE ASSEMBLY OF CALIFORNIA
(SHORT TITLE: OPERATION VOTE-SWITCH)

Ralph D. Morrell, Chairman
825 Newgate Way, Dixon, California 95620
Phone (916) 673-1697 2543

June 3, 1983

Mr. Daniel Stanford, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

"URGENT"

Dear Chairman Stanford:

On April 18, 1983 we received Mr. Paul Gann's letter dated April 15,
a copy of which is attached, together with the "flyer" captioned "Legislative
Reform Act", which we believe to be annotated in Gann's handwriting.

We have sent Gann several messages demanding he desist from falsely
representing his "Legislative Reform Act of 1983" would "eliminate" vote
switching, since in fact, the "Act" attempts to give statutory legitimacy
to the practice, through a four-fifths vote of the house. (See proposed
Section 9925)

We received today via U. S. Mail, the attached undated Gann letter,
together with a copy of the "Initiative" and a fund solicitation document,
also attached.

We submit Gann's action in his false assertion is a violation of
California's Political Reform Act, and due to the exigency of the situation,
request FPPC immediate telephonic action to cause a halt to continuance of
this claim, together with a public announcement as appropriate.

Additionally, Gann's solicitation of funds through the mail under
mantle of this falsehood appears to violate U. S. Postal regulations
pertaining to obtaining funds under false pretenses, U. S. Code, Title 18,
Sec. 1341.

Accordingly, we provide a copy of this letter and attachments to
the U. S. Postal Service, requesting their prompt action and advice in
the premise.

Sincerely,

Ralph D. Morrell, Chmn.
Operation Vote Switch

Attachments:
Gann ltr. Apr. 15, 1983 w/attachment.
Copy of "Initiative", annotated.
Copy of fund solicitation card.
Copy of Gann's undated letter captioned
"a special project of Citizens' Committee
to stop crime"

cc:
Inspector in Charge
U. S. Postal Service
W/attachments.

Paul Gann
People's Advocate
Incorporated

(916) 482-6173

Mailing Address                                                Business Address

P.O. Box 596 • Carmichael, California 95608          1485 Arden Way • Sacramento, California 95825


April 15, 1983


Mr. Ralph Morrell
825 Newgate Way
Dixon, CA

Dear Ralph:

I am sorry you did not get a complete copy of the Legislative Reform Act
of 1983. Enclosed you will find a newly revised copy. We filed it with
the Attorney General office on April 1, 1983.

Your concern of a court challenge is well taken; however, every initiative
I have ever sponsored has been challenged in the courts, so there's
nothing new from that point of view.

I thank you for past favors and hope we can work together in the future.

Sincerely,

Paul Gann
Paul Gann

Enclosure

PG/sl

LEGISLATIVE REFORM ACT

o Requires the Legislature to reduce the amount of money it
spends on itself by 30% or $16 million, bringing the growth in
Legislative spending since Proposition 13 more into line with
the growth in state spending in other areas.

o Prohibits the Legislature from increasing its own budget in
future years faster than the overall increase in state General
Fund spending.

o Requires an annual audit of the Legislative contingent funds by
an independent outside auditing firm.

o Requires the Legislature to make a more frequent and more
timely public disclosure of its spending practices.

o Prohibits powerful individual legislators from exercising
unilateral control over how and where the Legislature's money
is spent.

o Strips the Speaker of the Assembly and President pro Tempore of
the Senate of their power to exercise undue influence over the
fate of legislation by vesting their current powers in
bipartisan Rules Committees.

o Insures every lawmaker of the staff and resources needed to
adequately represent his constituents by requiring funding and
staffing in the Legislature to be allocated on a bipartisan
basis.

o Reestablishes the recently broken 132-year-old tradition of
bipartisan cooperation in running the Legislature by requiring
a 2/3 vote to adopt rules for each house.

o Insures that all legislative committees reflect the views and
interests of the Legislature in general by requiring that
committee membership and staffing be allocated on a bipartisan
basis.

o Eliminates ghost voting, vote switching, voting in the absence
of a quorum, and other abusive voting practices.

o Prevents unscrupulous lawmakers from sneaking bills into law by
requiring that all conference committee reports be public and
that all conference committee reports be printed and available
to the public before they can be voted into law.

[illegible]
[illegible]
[illegible]
[illegible]

INITIATIVE MEASURE TO BE SUBMITTED DIRECTLY TO THE VOTERS

The Attorney General of California has prepared the following title and summary of the chief purpose and points of the proposed measure:

LEGISLATURE: RULES, PROCEDURES, POWERS, FUNDING. INITIATIVE STATUTE.
Specifies that membership on Senate and Assembly Rules committees shall consist of members from two largest parties and accords largest party a one vote majority. Specifies that membership on other house legislative committees shall be proportional to partisan composition in each house. Specifies that each house and specified legislative committees approve, among other things, by two-thirds vote, rules, committee establishment, appointments by Speaker and disbursement of funds. Reduces Legislature's support appropriations by 30%; limits future support appropriations, and requires specified public reports and audits. Specifies other procedural, operational, staffing and funding requirements. Summary of estimate by Legislative Analyst and Director of Finance of fiscal impact on state and local governments: Assuming that this measure is presented to the voters on the June 1984 ballot, and that its initial fiscal impact occurs in the 1984-85 fiscal year, we estimate that enactment of the measure would reduce state General Fund expenditures by approximately $37 million in 1984-85, and reductions of roughly the same magnitude in subsequent fiscal years. This further assumes that the amounts appropriated for the Legislature in 1983-84 are equal to the amounts proposed in the Governor's Budget for 1983-84.

Article 7 — LEGISLATIVE POWERS AND DUTIES

SEC. 9600. [text appears to continue but becomes difficult to read with certainty in the image]

[The document continues with multiple sections of legislative text, including SEC. 9601, 9602, 9603, 9604, 9605, 9606, 9607, 9608, 9609, 9610 and beyond. The text becomes increasingly difficult to read clearly in the lower portions of the image.]

Article 4 — LEGISLATIVE FUNDS AND ADMINISTRATION

[This section continues with SEC. 9630 and subsequent sections detailing legislative funding and administrative procedures]

SEC. 9634. Contingent Availability of Funds Specified. [Final section visible in image]

Important!

Your stamp will save CCSC
the cost of postage.
Thank you.

BUSINESS REPLY MAIL
FIRST CLASS PERMIT NO. 5893 SACRAMENTO CA 95813

POSTAGE WILL BE PAID BY ADDRESSEE

Citizen's Committee to Stop Crime
P.O. Box 13299
Sacramento, CA 95813

NO POSTAGE
NECESSARY
IF MAILED
IN THE
UNITED STATES

THIS OFFICIAL DOCUMENT
HAS BEEN PERSONALLY
REGISTERED TO:

157

RALPH MORRELL
825 NEUGATE HY
DIXON, CA 95620

occupation

business address

place of business (if self employed)

Attention: Paul Gann

I agree that we must pass the Legislative Reform
Act of 1983.

☐ I have returned my signed petition.
☐ I have enclosed my urgently needed contribution for:
$50 _____ $25 _____ $15 _____ Other _____
☐ Rush more petitions to me for my friends and
neighbors.

Make checks payable to the Legislative Reform Project.

Legislative Reform
Act of 1983

a special project of
Citizens' Committee to Stop Crime


Dear Friend,

     Did you know that in the last four years the Politicians who
control our California Legislature have increased spending on
themselves from $60 million to over $121 million?

     That's an 103% increase since Proposition 13 was passed!

     It's also more than a million dollars per legislator per year,
not counting their salaries...which by the way they are attempting
to raise by 20%.

     At the same time, these very politicians are telling us that
police protection, fire protection and other vital services must
be cut back because of "lack of funds".

     Did you know that the Chairmen of the Senate and Assembly
Rules Committees have the power to spend millions of taxpayer
dollars any way they choose, without public scrutiny or knowledge?

     Or did you know that a legislator can create a bill in the
last few hours of the legislative session...avoiding committee
hearings, public knowledge or close analysis...and slide it through
the legislature into law?

     And did you know that currently a legislator can vote one way
on a bill, then later change his vote so as to mislead the people
he is supposed to represent?

HAVE YOU HEARD ENOUGH?

     I hope so...because you can help put a stop to these practices
right now by signing and returning the enclosed LEGAL PETITION for
the LEGISLATIVE REFORM ACT OF 1983.

     The LEGISLATIVE REFORM ACT will solve these problems, but it
will do something else possibly even more important.

     It will make sure that a small group of Liberal legislators
cannot continue to control the entire legislative process.

     Let me explain how this has come about.

     Just after the Legislature convened for the 1983-84 Legislative
session, the Democrats changed a 132 year old tradition which required a 2/3 vote for rules changes in the legislature.

                                                            over...

THAT GIVES THEM COMPLETE CONTROL OVER THE ENTIRE LEGISLATIVE
PROCESS!

The first thing they did was to throw ALL REPUBLICANS OUT
AS CHAIRMEN OR VICE-CHAIRMEN OF ALL COMMITTEES IN THE ASSEMBLY.

The Republicans were quickly replaced by Liberal Democrats
who helped Assembly Speaker Willie Brown during the 1982
elections.

Then, in an unbelievable move, Speaker Brown and his allies
stripped away much of the staff and funding from the Republicans
in the Assembly.

Leaving them with little funds or staff to serve even their
own constituents.

Many people call the Speaker of the Assembly the second most
powerful man in California, next to the Governor.

They are wrong. Today, Willie Brown is the most powerful
man in California!

And in the State Senate, where President David Roberti rules
with an iron hand, things are not much better.

The power of these two men has gotten so out of control
that many legislators, Democrats and Republicans alike, recognize
it as a serious problem.

But few are willing to come out front and help for fear of what
Willie Brown or David Roberti would do to them in the next election.

So I have once again taken up the banner of legislative change
through the initiative process, by introducing the LEGISLATIVE
REFORM ACT OF 1983.

Here's what the Act would do if passed;

1. It will reduce the State Legislators' budget by over $36
million bringing it more into line with other areas of
state spending.

2. It will give every legislator, regardless of party, the
staff and funding to adequately represent the people by
requiring funding and staff to be allocated on a pro-
portional basis.

3. Re-establishes that any rules changes in the Legislature
be by a 2/3 vote to ensure that our elected representatives
agree any change is in the best interests of the voters.

4. It limits the power of the President of the Senate and
the Speaker of the Assembly to exercise influence over
any and all legislation.

over...

5.    Requires that both parties have fair representation on
all Committees in the Senate and Assembly.

6.    Prohibit "vote switching" and "ghost voting" which have
allowed members of the Legislature to mask their true
actions from the people they claim to represent.

     Once again, all voters in California will be represented in
our state government.

     Once again, tough anti-crime laws and tax reform laws will
have a chance of passage in the Legislature.

     I am willing to do my part to make sure that it happens.

     But you have to be willing to do your part.

     It's the only way we can win.

     And here's what it will take to win:

          You must immediately sign and get any other registered
     voters you can to sign the enclosed petition.

          There is a separate instruction and contribution reply
     sheet which gives you exact details on how to fill out
     your petition.

     READ IT CAREFULLY AND FOLLOW THE DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS.

     Send a desperately needed contribution to help carry
more petitions to other voters.

     You do not have to contribute in order to return your signed
petition.

     But please understand that unless most citizens who sign
petitions also send a contribution we simply will not be able to
send out enough petitions to qualify this initiative.

     I am asking for a one-time contribution of $25.

     If you are deciding whether or not you can contribute, I want
you to think of one very important point.

     We are on the verge of letting our votes become meaningless...
...in a state where the Speaker of the Assembly and the President of
the Senate rule with absolute power over all legislators and voters
alike.

     I'm sure you agree with me that the legislative leaders of either
party, be they Democrat or Republican, shouldn't have total power over
our state government.

     You can bet that a major attack on the Legislative Reform Act
will take place.  Willie Brown and David Roberti have already made
comments to that effect.

                                                          over...

UNBELIEVABLY, THEY'VE HIRED A HIGH POWERED SAN FRANCISCO LAW
FIRM AT TAXPAYERS' EXPENSE TO FIGHT OUR INITIATIVE.

That's why it's going to take the financial help of you and
most of the other voters I write to.

Maybe you can't afford to send $25 at this time.

You may only be able to send $10 or $15.

You may be one of those who can afford to send $100 or even
$500.

The important thing is that you help by sending whatever amount
you feel you can afford. It's the only way we have a chance of
getting the Initiative passed and on the ballot.

The Citizens' Committee to Stop Crime has already committed
over $170,000 to the Legislative Reform Act of 1983.

I'm sure that you can see we have made a major commitment
to making this initiative a success.

And unless I can count on your financial support, along with the
support of thousands of other citizens, every penny spent so far
will have been wasted.

I pray you will not let that happen.

This is, without a doubt, the most important initiative campaign
you and I have ever been involved in.

We have been able to cut back on many wasteful areas of California's state government over the last four years. And we have
been able to make meaningful reforms in the area of crime.

It's time we helped clean up the legislature's own backyard,
since the politicians refuse to do it themselves. And we will save
over $36 million at the same time.

Please send your completed petition along with your most generous
contribution now while you are thinking about it.

Thank you,

[signature]
Paul Gann

P.S. There are five signature spaces on your Petition. Please
do all you can to get signatures in each space. And please,
PLEASE send your contribution of $25 or more to help pay for
this whole project. Our fate is in YOUR HANDS. Thank you.
